title: Georgia

jokes.text: mad-lab-blah. - Madloba.
jokes.setup: Selina attempting to say Georgian 'thank you' properly
jokes.punchline: Complete butchering of the pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Selina's obliviousness and inability to handle foreign cultures gracefully

jokes.text: Jesus, democracy. What a horror show this is.
jokes.setup: Just finished promoting democracy in official capacity
jokes.punchline: Immediate private dismissal of democracy as horror show
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Selina's cynicism and the show's political satire. Excellent structure and economy.

jokes.text: I didn't even know Georgia was a country.
jokes.setup: Former president on international democracy mission
jokes.punchline: Admits basic geographic ignorance
jokes.explanation: Perfectly in character - Selina's ignorance masquerading as honesty.

jokes.text: Hello! One day, you can grow up to be president. No, not you. Your brother.
jokes.setup: Inspiring a young girl about becoming president
jokes.punchline: Immediate sexist correction to her brother
jokes.explanation: Sharp misdirection that reveals cultural attitudes. Good structure.

jokes.text: Unbelievable.
jokes.punchline: Perfectly timed deadpan reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: This place sucks my ghost nard.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre made-up vulgar expression
jokes.explanation: Highly original Selina-ism. Creative vulgar neologism that's surprisingly quotable.

jokes.text: Hawaii is rightfully a monarchy and will be again.
jokes.setup: Selina complaining about not getting Hawaii assignment
jokes.punchline: Completely serious absurd political stance
jokes.explanation: Unexpected absurd political position delivered deadpan. Good economy.

jokes.text: spreading democracy like patient zero
jokes.setup: Describing her role in spreading democracy
jokes.punchline: Comparing herself to the first person to spread a disease
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark metaphor that perfectly captures the show's cynical view of American foreign policy

jokes.text: Sex trafficking workshop. / It's an anti-sex trafficking workshop. / Lame.
jokes.setup: Reading schedule, excited about 'sex trafficking workshop'
jokes.punchline: Disappointed it's ANTI-sex trafficking
jokes.explanation: Dark misdirection perfectly executed. Reveals Selina's moral bankruptcy through misunderstanding.

jokes.text: Look at us, just like the good, old days except shittier in every conceivable way.
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic sentiment immediately undercut
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina structure - false sentiment immediately demolished. Good character work.

jokes.text: Maybe we can win an election for a change. / That came out wrong. / Yeah, way wrong, right?
jokes.setup: Trying to be optimistic about their prospects
jokes.punchline: Immediate recognition of the bitter irony
jokes.explanation: Self-aware acknowledgment of their electoral failures. In character but not exceptional.

jokes.text: It's gotta be 8:00 AM somewhere.
jokes.punchline: Twist on 'it's 5 o'clock somewhere' but making it worse
jokes.explanation: Decent twist on familiar saying that shows Selina's drinking problem

jokes.text: Jailbait statesman.
jokes.punchline: Dark sexual twist on the phrase
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with dark undertones. Excellent economy, though somewhat gratuitous.

jokes.text: Night and fog. Nacht und Nebel.
jokes.punchline: Casual Nazi reference to describe their situation
jokes.explanation: Dark historical reference fits Ben's cynical worldview. Character-appropriate but not structurally brilliant.

jokes.text: I will destroy you in ways that are so creative, they will honor me for it at the Kennedy Center.
jokes.punchline: Threat escalated to artistic achievement level
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that turns vengeance into high art. Perfectly captures Selina's grandiosity and vindictiveness.

jokes.text: I am gonna find ways to destroy you so hard that everybody at the Kennedy Center is gonna take a fucking massive shit.
jokes.punchline: Vulgar escalation of the Kennedy Center callback
jokes.explanation: Callback that escalates with vulgarity but loses the elegance of the original. Still in character.

jokes.text: I already have 434 brand-new best friends and we're all going to dinner tonight. And you're not invited. Sorry. Congressmen only.
jokes.punchline: Childish exclusion disguised as congressional privilege
jokes.explanation: Reveals Ryan's petty vindictiveness through playground logic. Competent character work.

jokes.text: I got a Georgian SIM card. Got text forwarding. I'll here from you?
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'hear' as 'here'
jokes.explanation: Basic Richard malapropism, though not particularly creative

jokes.text: That's not the Buddy Calhoun I see in the mirror. Although, it would be if you stepped out of the shower.
jokes.setup: Buddy defending his dignity after exposure incident
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating admission about his appearance
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor that acknowledges his physical shortcomings. Decent structure and economy.

jokes.text: I'm happiest when Buddy and I are on the couch eating popcorn and watching 'Downton Abbey.' / Can we play that again?
jokes.setup: Amy trying to deflect from embarrassing video
jokes.punchline: Host immediately requesting to replay the humiliating footage
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment where Amy's attempt at damage control backfires immediately.

jokes.text: Ames, who's your favorite character on 'Downton'? I don't know. Abby, I guess. Yep.
jokes.setup: Danny testing Amy's knowledge of the show she claims to love
jokes.punchline: Amy reveals she doesn't actually watch it by naming non-existent character 'Abby'
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment exposing Amy's media manipulation lies. The specific wrong answer makes it more painful and funny.

jokes.text: I don't know. Abby, I guess.
jokes.setup: Host asking about her favorite Downton Abbey character
jokes.punchline: Obvious lack of knowledge about the show she just claimed to love
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy moment - caught in a lie and handling it poorly. Great economy and character work.

jokes.text: You have a doctrine now? What is it, 'Boners are rare, don't waste them'?
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual interpretation of his political impotence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dual meaning - political impotence and literal sexual dysfunction. Classic Selina vulgarity with wit.

jokes.text: It was a spa. / No, no, a spa is where you go to get a massage and the like. You were in an insane asylum.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to reframe her mental health treatment
jokes.punchline: Minna's blunt correction and insistence on accuracy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where Minna refuses to let Selina save face. Solid structure.

jokes.text: We are, in fact, lovers. / What? / Lovers. / Lovers. / With Scab Calloway?
jokes.setup: Minna revealing her relationship with Nikolai
jokes.punchline: Selina's horrified nickname for the disfigured candidate
jokes.explanation: Brutal nickname that reveals Selina's cruelty. Good escalating discomfort.

jokes.text: I went to find a hotel room, right? Nobody speaks English here, everything was really confusing to me, and I was chased by children. And I think I may have voted.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of accidentally voting in foreign election
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike moment - bumbling incompetence escalating to major international incident. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Mike, that is the kind of voting irregularity that we are here to prevent.
jokes.setup: Mike admitting to voting in Georgian election
jokes.punchline: Kent's dry observation of the irony
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent deadpan that highlights the absurd irony. Great character voice and structure.

jokes.text: I accidentally voted and now my thumb is green. - The same thing happened to me! Oh, my God! - Oh, my God, oh, my God.
jokes.punchline: Gary had the same accident, both panicking
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing both characters' incompetence

jokes.text: The same thing happened to me! Oh, my God! / Oh, my God, oh, my God. / How did it happen?
jokes.punchline: Richard also accidentally voted, shared panic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing multiple team members compromising the election. Solid character work.

jokes.text: I can't afford to lose this job, Gary, but I can't afford to keep it either. I mean, it so expensive to commute from DC. And I haven't been paid yet.
jokes.setup: Mike worried about losing his job
jokes.punchline: Revealing the impossible financial situation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mike's perpetual financial struggles

jokes.text: I never ever wanna see the inside of your mouth again. Okay? It looks like a Haitian porta-potty in there.
jokes.setup: Gary eating with his mouth open
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and offensive comparison
jokes.explanation: Offensively specific imagery that's both horrible and memorable. Classic Selina cruelty.

jokes.text: Hey, Gary, can I sleep on your couch? - No. - Hard no? - It's a hard no.
jokes.punchline: Gary's immediate and firm refusal
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Gary's selfishness despite their friendship

jokes.text: Yeah, but do we really want these people to have electricity?
jokes.setup: Discussion about expensive caviar helping villages
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of entire population's needs
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Selina's callous elitism. Excellent economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Jesus, you and Doyle really have a wide-on for this guy.
jokes.punchline: Vulgar female version of 'hard-on'
jokes.explanation: Creative vulgar neologism that works both linguistically and characteristically. Good economy.

jokes.text: Well, it's a miracle that anybody survives eating sushi in this country, Minna.
jokes.setup: Minna explaining poisoning attempt at sushi restaurant
jokes.punchline: Selina's dismissive observation about Georgian food safety
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that deflates the dramatic poisoning story. Solid structure.

jokes.text: I cannot possibly accept that. - I mean, even the appearance of a quid pro quo...
jokes.punchline: Selina's performative refusal while clearly considering it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's internal conflict between ethics and greed

jokes.text: As clear as the coffee table Danny Thomas had his hookers shit on. / It's an American expression.
jokes.setup: Murman saying he's made himself clear
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up disgusting 'American expression'
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurdist creation. Perfectly captures Selina making up cultural 'facts' with complete confidence.

jokes.text: I will give you my sperm. / Oh, great! / Yeah. / I thought that would take a lot longer.
jokes.setup: Jonah asking for help having a baby
jokes.punchline: Dan immediately volunteering sperm, Jonah's surprise at easy agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Dan's immediate sexual availability and Jonah's naive surprise.

jokes.text: Okay, uh, could we start the clock in, like, 30 minutes? / Yeah, I guess so. / Actually, no. No, make it 40. The girl I'm thinking of likes to talk first.
jokes.setup: Being asked not to ejaculate for 72 hours
jokes.punchline: Negotiating time for immediate sexual encounter with specific details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan character work - turning medical advice into immediate sexual logistics. Great escalation.

jokes.text: I'd kill for a job where I could wear flats all day.
jokes.punchline: Selina's envy of comfortable work shoes
jokes.explanation: Good relatable moment that humanizes Selina slightly

jokes.text: That's keep a lot of docents in Rockports. / I'd kill for a job where I could wear flats all day.
jokes.punchline: Specific observation about comfortable museum shoes
jokes.explanation: Great specific observational humor about museum culture. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: There is literally no Georgian law. / And I'm using 'literally' correctly.
jokes.punchline: Meta-comment about proper word usage
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about language misuse while making a serious point. Perfect Ben pedantry.

jokes.text: There is literally no Georgian law. And I'm using 'literally' correctly.
jokes.punchline: Ben's pedantic clarification about word usage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben moment combining political cynicism with linguistic pedantry

jokes.text: Yeah, that and a car with a sunroof could've bought you my virginity in '83. / You were 22? / No, I was 15 in 1983.
jokes.punchline: Selina's casual admission of underage prostitution
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that escalates unexpectedly. Reveals Selina's casual relationship with shocking admissions.

jokes.text: Yeah, that and a car with a sunroof could've bought you my virginity in '83. - You were 22? - No, I was 15 in 1983.
jokes.setup: Minna saying Nikolai has soul of a poet
jokes.punchline: Selina's crude reference to teenage prostitution, then age correction
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good structure and reveals character backstory

jokes.text: Yeah, that and a car with a sunroof could've bought you my virginity in '83. - You were 22?
jokes.setup: Crude comment about past sexual standards
jokes.punchline: Minna's math revealing Selina was underage
jokes.explanation: Good setup that reveals uncomfortable math about Selina's age. The misdirection works well.

jokes.text: Did he throw himself from a moving town car? / No. No, he weighted his boots and he jumped into an ice hole.
jokes.punchline: Selina's flippant guess vs. the bizarre reality
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy where reality is stranger than Selina's callous assumption. Good structure.

jokes.text: Your noses must've been really tiny yesterday because you lied and now they're normal-sized.
jokes.punchline: Backwards Pinocchio logic implying they're liars
jokes.explanation: Clever reverse logic but feels forced. Decent wordplay but not exceptional.

jokes.text: In the immortal words of will.i.am, 'Tonight's gonna be really great!'
jokes.punchline: Treating pop star as philosophical source
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ryan's try-hard nature

jokes.text: In the immortal words of will.i.am, 'Tonight's gonna be really great!'
jokes.punchline: Misattributing bland statement to will.i.am
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan moment showing his cultural disconnect. The mundane 'quote' attributed to will.i.am is absurd.

jokes.text: Fuck. - Are you fucking...?! - That's where that goes? - Pick up your fucking chairs!
jokes.punchline: Physical comedy and angry reactions
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Oh, you must remember not to react to his disfigurement. He's very sensitive. - Wait, how bad is it, exactly? - Oh, well, he was very handsome before the poisoning, so you may not even notice it.
jokes.punchline: Contradictory advice about noticing disfigurement
jokes.explanation: Good setup for visual gag with contradictory logic

jokes.text: May I say that the reports of your beauty have not been exaggerated. / Well, back at you, red.
jokes.punchline: Selina's casual reference to his disfigurement
jokes.explanation: Brutal Selina response to polite flattery. Good contrast and character work.

jokes.text: Well, back at you, red.
jokes.punchline: Selina's awkward response to his disfigurement
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Selina's inability to handle awkward situations

jokes.text: I mean, you know, who needs all that champagne when you can have all these different kinds of wallpaper?
jokes.setup: Nikolai contrasting his humble home with Murman's palaces
jokes.punchline: Selina's sarcastic appreciation of his tacky decor
jokes.explanation: Good observational sarcasm about pretentious simplicity. Solid Selina voice.

jokes.text: I mean, you know, who needs all that champagne when you can have all these different kinds of wallpaper?
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically praising tacky wallpaper
jokes.explanation: Good Selina sarcasm about tasteless decoration

jokes.text: His very touch makes me quiver. / Okay, and now we get to eat food.
jokes.setup: Minna describing her sexual relationship with Nikolai
jokes.punchline: Selina's desperate topic change
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe moment with perfect Selina escape attempt. Good economy and timing.

jokes.text: Eat the soup. / Why? / Because I'm hungry. I need to know if it's been poisoned.
jokes.setup: Selina ordering Ben to eat soup
jokes.punchline: Using him as poison tester while claiming hunger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - using subordinate as poison tester with transparent excuse. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Oh, it has carrots in it. I'm not gonna eat that.
jokes.setup: After Ben tested soup for poison
jokes.punchline: Selina rejecting it for trivial reason
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline showing Selina's pettiness after paranoid poison testing

jokes.text: I agree. It is so much less than you are worth.
jokes.punchline: Nikolai agreeing but for opposite reason - it's too low
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that escalates the corruption. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: To ensure fair election, I would like to offer you a $15 million bribe. - How dare you, sir!
jokes.punchline: Selina's performative outrage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with obvious performative morality

jokes.text: but unbelievably shitty apartment.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Nikolai's wealth vs. living space
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of his home
jokes.explanation: Direct Selina observation that's both tactless and accurate. The bluntness is in character.

jokes.text: If you told him that rava ganavlis was chakapuli, he would eat it up and ask for seconds.
jokes.punchline: Foreign language food deception
jokes.explanation: Setup for the scatological revelation. Works as cultural humor but needs the payoff.

jokes.text: If you told him that rava ganavlis was chakapuli, he would eat it up and ask for seconds. / I can only hope that rava ganavlis is human shit. / It is actually what you vomit after eating shit.
jokes.setup: Nikolai insulting Doyle's gullibility with Georgian terms
jokes.punchline: Escalating disgust through specific bodily function definitions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with specific disgusting imagery. Works but relies on shock value.

jokes.text: You know, the band's name translates to Panzer Division. - What?
jokes.punchline: Revealing they're at a Nazi-themed concert
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Richard's oblivious delivery

jokes.text: The poison? It did not engorge only his face, if you know what I mean. / I think I do. / It also engorged his penis and made it very unusual texture of sea cucumber, so it's great for vaginal orgasms.
jokes.setup: Minna hinting at sexual enhancement from poisoning
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of texture and function
jokes.explanation: Overly graphic shock humor that lacks the wit of better Veep jokes. Crude but not clever.

jokes.text: You know, I just come and get it over with.
jokes.punchline: Selina's brutally pragmatic approach to sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina line - reducing intimacy to pure efficiency. Excellent economy and character work.

jokes.text: Which do you prefer, vaginal or clitoral orgasms? - You know, I just come and get it over with.
jokes.punchline: Selina's matter-of-fact sexual efficiency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina response showing her pragmatic approach to everything, even sex

jokes.text: You two ding-dongs look like you fingered the Incredible Hulk.
jokes.setup: Mike and Richard with green-stained thumbs from voting
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual metaphor with comic book reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific visual metaphor that's both vulgar and clever. Great economy and originality.

jokes.text: Murman's leading by more votes than there are people in the country. - Yeah, maybe he's bussing them in from Chicago.
jokes.punchline: Reference to American election fraud conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Good political humor referencing American election conspiracy theories

jokes.text: This election's going down like Eleanor Roosevelt at Dinah Shore Weekend.
jokes.punchline: Obscure lesbian reference about going down
jokes.explanation: Brilliant obscure reference with double entendre that's very Selina

jokes.text: Well, Murman, that is a big tire with a fuckload of fish in it.
jokes.setup: Murman's increased offer referencing his earlier story
jokes.punchline: Selina using his metaphor to acknowledge the larger bribe
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Selina engaging with the corruption using Murman's own metaphor.

jokes.text: Well, Murman, that is a big tire with a fuckload of fish in it.
jokes.punchline: Reference to his earlier story metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier story with Selina's characteristic vulgarity

jokes.text: My concern is I wonder if your judgment is being clouded by your feelings that are brought on by Nikolai's lumpy poison cock.
jokes.setup: Questioning Minna's objectivity about the election
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of the sexual influence
jokes.explanation: Crude but direct addressing of the conflict of interest. Less witty than Selina's best insults.

jokes.text: Yeah, in Finland, we call this the fever of the sausage.
jokes.setup: Discussion of being clouded by sexual feelings
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous Finnish cultural reference
jokes.explanation: Amusing cultural absurdity that fits Minna's character. Good economy and originality.

jokes.text: Sometimes I don't feel worthy of this friendship. / Mm, well, sometimes I feel that way, too.
jokes.setup: Minna expressing gratitude for their friendship
jokes.punchline: Selina agreeing that Minna isn't worthy
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that reveals Selina's casual cruelty. Excellent structure and economy.

jokes.text: Wow, that's like Gary becoming president. / From the tea party. / No. Oh, yeah, that is funny.
jokes.setup: Comparing unlikely political rise to Gary's prospects
jokes.punchline: Gary's misunderstanding and Selina's delayed cruel agreement
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics showing Gary's confusion and Selina's cruelty. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: It's now worth approximately $389,000. Way to go, ma'am.
jokes.punchline: Kent's sarcastic congratulations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent deadpan delivery highlighting the complete failure

jokes.text: Way to go, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Library fund losing massive value due to coup
jokes.punchline: Gary's inappropriately cheerful congratulations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary obliviousness to disaster. Good character work and economy.

jokes.text: And that, in a nutshell, is what I call the Meyer Doctrine.
jokes.setup: Claiming credit for democratic progress after causing chaos
jokes.punchline: Grandiose naming of her 'doctrine' after complete failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending showing Selina's delusional self-importance. Good callback and character integration.

tmdb_overview: Selina helps monitor the first free and democratic election in Georgia (the country, not the state) and is reunited with an old "friend." Meanwhile, Mike and Gary hide evidence of a major mishap, and Jonah and Richard have an eventful night out. Back in the U.S., Dan tries to help Amy.
insight: Political chaos fuels 80 jokes in 36 minutes—Veep's densest episode yet.
